CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2014 Set For Trial On Jun-08-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Filed By Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Aug-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D501)

Appearances:
Attorney Hannah Salassi of LVOVICH VOLCHEGURSKY & SZUCSKO (415-392-2560), counsel for plaintiff/moving party
Attorney Ann Rankin, Esq. (510-653-8886), counsel for defendants/opposing parties HOA and individuals
Attorney Paul Glaspy of Glaspy & Glaspy (408-279-8844), counsel for individual defendants/opposing parties

Court hears arguments from both sides. The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Filed By Plaintiffs is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden. IT Corp. v. County of Imperial (1983) 35 Cal.3d. 63, 69-72. Plaintiffs did not prove that they are likely to prevail on the merits. Defendants have presented sufficient evidence of a properly held vote in levying the $1,200,000 assessment. Furthermore, Plaintiffs failed to show that they will suffer an irreparable harm. A written order is signed and filed in open court.

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Filed By Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Jul-28-2014 Continued To Aug-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is Continued to August 15, 2014. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers to department 501 no later than July 31, 2014 by 4 p.m. with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Not Reported. (AJR/501/rg)
